      if (window.ccc && window.ccc.consent) {
        // Update consent based on user preferences
        gtag('consent', 'update', {

      requestAnimationFrame(function() {
        requestAnimationFrame(function() {
          var banner = document.querySelector('.ccc-banner');

          }

  <!-- UTM parameter handling for forms -->
  <script>
    function getUTMParameterFromCookie(parameterName) {
      // Get the cookie value
      const cookies = document.cookie.split(';');
      let gtmCampaignURL = null;

      for (let i = 0; i < cookies.length; i += 1) {
        const cookie = cookies[i].trim();
        if (cookie.startsWith('__gtm_campaign_url=')) {
          gtmCampaignURL = decodeURIComponent(cookie.substring('__gtm_campaign_url='.length));
          break;
        }
      }

      if (!gtmCampaignURL) {
        return null;
      }

      // Parse the URL to extract the specified query parameter
      const url = new URL(gtmCampaignURL);
      const parameterValue = url.searchParams.get(parameterName);

      return parameterValue || 'null';
    }

    // Retrieve UTM parameter values
    const utmSource = getUTMParameterFromCookie('utm_source');
    const utmMedium = getUTMParameterFromCookie('utm_medium');
    const utmCampaign = getUTMParameterFromCookie('utm_campaign');
    const utmContent = getUTMParameterFromCookie('utm_content');
    const utmTerm = getUTMParameterFromCookie('utm_term');

    // Set the values in the OLAF form inputs if they exist
    const formInputUtmSource = document.getElementById('form-input-utm_source');
    if (formInputUtmSource) {
      formInputUtmSource.value = utmSource || 'null';
    }

    const formInputUtmMedium = document.getElementById('form-input-utm_medium');
    if (formInputUtmMedium) {
      formInputUtmMedium.value = utmMedium || 'null';
    }

    const formInputUtmCampaign = document.getElementById('form-input-utm_campaign');
    if (formInputUtmCampaign) {
      formInputUtmCampaign.value = utmCampaign || 'null';
    }

    const formInputUtmContent = document.getElementById('form-input-utm_content');
    if (formInputUtmContent) {
      formInputUtmContent.value = utmContent || 'null';
    }

    const formInputUtmTerm = document.getElementById('form-input-utm_term');
    if (formInputUtmTerm) {
      formInputUtmTerm.value = utmTerm || 'null';
    }
  </script>

 Join Our Newsletter

Caron in Pennsylvania

 
 

 1-800-854-6023 
 

 
 

 

 
Caron in Florida

 
 

 1-800-221-6500 
 

 
 

 

 
Breakthrough at Caron

 
 

 1-800-213-7834 
 

 
 

 

 
Caron Atlanta Outpatient Center

 
 

 678-543-5718 
 

 
 

 

 
Caron Philadelphia Outpatient Center

 
 

 484-342-5750 
 

 
 

 

 
Encore Outpatient Services

 
 

 703-436-8158 
 

 
 

 

 
Caron Outpatient Treatment Center

 
 

 484-345-4670 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 
Transforming Lives Through Addiction Treatment

 

Caron’s expert staff has helped thousands of people and their families transform their lives, combining neurorestorative care with proven treatment modalities.

 

 
 Start your journey today
 
 

 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 
 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 115k 
 

 
 

 
Patients Served Since 2000

 
 

 
Immersive treatment programs designed for sustainable sobriety

 

 

 

 
 

 

 70 
 

 
 

 
Years of Results

 
 

 
Caron’s been setting standards and delivering real evidence-based treatment since 1957

 

 

 

 
 

 

 90% 
 

 
 

 
of Patients

 
 

 
rated their quality of life as good to very good a year after treatment at Caron

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 
 
Programs

 
 

 

 Comprehensive care for substance use and co-occurring mental health, offering personalized treatment and lasting recovery solutions.
 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 
 

 
Inpatient treatment

 
 
Patients live at the treatment facility with a structured environment and 24/7 support.

 
 
 Learn more about Inpatient treatment 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 
 

 
Outpatient Treatment

 
 
Allows patients to receive treatment and support while living at home.

 
 
 Learn more about Outpatient Treatment 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 
 

 
Family programs

 
 
Dedicated resources for families before, during and after treatment.

 
 
 Learn more about Family programs 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 
 

 
Resources & Support

 
 
Individualized services for you and your loved one to successfully transition back to everyday life.

 
 
 Learn More about Resources & Support 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 
 
Insurance

 
 

 

 Caron has negotiated in-network contracts with several insurance providers for both inpatient and outpatient care.
 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 Our insurance options
 
 

 

 

 
 

 
 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Pennsylvania

 
 
Caron’s Pennsylvania campus provides inpatient addiction and behavioral health treatment for individuals 16+ with specialized programs for LGBTAIQ+, Healthcare Professionals, executives and more.

 
 
 See Pennsylvania
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Florida

 
 
Caron’s Florida campus provides inpatient and outpatient treatment for addiction and behavioral health for individuals 18+ with specialized programs for affluent adults, Neurocognitive Assessments and professionals.

 
 
 See Florida
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
Ginny

 
 

 "Without Caron, my life would look very different. It would not be full of promise, and hope, and purpose, and honesty. And love."
 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
Steve

 
 

 I didn’t know ‘how’ to get well. And that’s what Caron did for me. They work the whole body. Caron transformed me into a well, healthy here [mind] and here [body] person.
 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
Louis and Stephanie

 
 

 That was like the biggest moment for me at Caron. Because I’m allowed to have feelings and I’m allowed to get upset and I’m allowed to feel like I matter in all of this. And not get in trouble for it.
 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 
 

 
See what others are saying

 
 

 Read our reviews today!
 

 

 

 
 

 
 Read reviews
 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 
 
Community

 
 

 

 Caron offers virtual and in-person recovery services to complement the work you did in treatment and navigate early recovery.
 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 
 

 
Recovery Services

 
 
Services are offered virtually and include My Recovery Connection.

 
 
 Learn more about Recovery Services about Recovery Services 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 
 

 
Alumni Community

 
 
Caron’s Alumni Community is a welcoming network of alumni who share their experiences, offer support, and celebrate life in recovery together.

 
 
 Learn more and Alumni Community about Alumni Community 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 
 

 
Parent and Family Support Groups

 
 
Groups to support loved ones of individuals with substance use disorder and to provide a safe place to find education and hope.

 
 
 Learn more about support groups about Parent and Family Support Groups 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 
 

 
Breakthrough at Caron

 
 
Caron’s Breakthrough Program offers transformative workshops for individuals, couples, and others.

 
 
 Learn more about Breakthrough about Breakthrough at Caron 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 
 
Upcoming events

 
 

 

 Engaging events supporting recovery, education, and community connection.
 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 View all events
 
 

 

 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 
20th Annual Greater New York Community Awards Breakfast

 
 

Wednesday, March 25th 8:30 AM - 10:30 AM | Mutual of America Building

 

 
 

 

 
 

 
 Event Details
 
 

 

 

 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 
Caron Atlanta 6th Annual Gala

 
 
 
 April 16, 2026 | Piedmont Driving Club

 

 

 
 

 
 Learn More
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 
Richard J. Caron Award of Excellence Celebration

 
 
 
 April 29, 2026 | DoubleTree by Hilton Hotel Reading

 

 

 
 

 
 Learn More
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 
2026 Caron's New York Gala

 
 
 
 June 2, 2026 | Cipriani 42nd Street

 

 

 
 

 
 Learn More
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 
 

 
Can’t attend an event? Here’s how to donate.

 
 

 Your donation directly supports life-changing treatment and recovery programs for those who need them most.
 

 

 

 
 

 
 Make a donation

<div class="flexible-content flexible-content--body">
  
                                                                                                                                                                                  <div class="wysiwyg container-fluid">
  <h2>Know your options to afford quality care. </h2>
<figure style="float:right;width:294px;max-width:294px;margin-left:20px;margin-right:20px;"><a href="https://www.caron.org/news-resources/americas-best-addiction-treatment-centers-2023"><img src="https://deln1jxmpfoj4.cloudfront.net/Logos/Media-logos/_medium/Newsweek_US-BATC2024_Logo_Basic.png" alt="America's Best Addiction Treatment Centers - Caron" width="294" height="364" /></a><figcaption> Click to learn more.</figcaption></figure>
<p>Getting treatment for a substance use disorder is not optional. That’s why Caron is committed to helping you find ways to pay for it. Because treatment is personalized for our patients, we do not have a set fee. Some of the variables in the cost of treatment include the <a href="https://www.caron.org/treatment-programs" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">specific treatment program that is right for you</a> and the additional services or support you need or choose.<br /></p>
<p>We recommend contacting an admissions specialist to discuss your situation and needs. Our specialists have in-depth knowledge of our programs and the payment options available. If Caron is not the right treatment center for you, we will help you find another quality program.<br /></p>
</div>
  

    

</div>
  
    
    
<div class="flexible-content flexible-content--body">
  
                                                                                                                                                                                  <div class="wysiwyg container-fluid">
  <h3>How to Pay</h3>
<p>We accept certified checks, cash, credit cards (American Express, MasterCard, Visa, and Discover), and wire transfers. Payments can be made upon arrival (with prior approval) or before you arrive by <a href="https://www.caron.org/make-a-payment" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">using our secure website</a> or <a href="https://www.caron.org/contact-us" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">contacting an admissions specialist</a>.</p>
</div>
  

    

</div>
  
    
    
<div class="flexible-content flexible-content--body">
  
                                                                                                                                                                                  <div class="wysiwyg container-fluid">
  <h3>Treatment Payment Options</h3>
<p>At Caron, we believe that everyone deserves <a href="https://www.caron.org/admissions/becoming-a-patient" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">access to treatment</a>. We partner with several <a href="https://www.caron.org/admissions/insurance">national and local insurance companies</a>, provide financing, and offer financial assistance to those who need it.</p>
</div>
  

    

</div>
  
    
    
<div class="flexible-content flexible-content--body">
  
                                                                                                                                                                                  <div class="wysiwyg container-fluid">
  <h3>Financing</h3>
<p>Caron has long-standing relationships with two lending companies (<a href="http://www.mlendfinance.com/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">M-lend Financial</a> and <a href="https://www.prosper.com/vib/healthcare/program_select?provider_id=003610&version=pad_ls" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">Prosper</a>) that help patients and families pay for treatment. They offer financing for one year or for two years depending upon your eligibility.</p>
<h3>Financial Assistance</h3>
<p>Caron provides financial assistance to patients who otherwise may not be able to afford treatment. To find out if you are eligible, please talk with one of our admissions specialists.</p>
</div>
  

    

</div>

        <script src='https://unpkg.com/focus-within-polyfill@5.2.1/dist/focus-within-polyfill.js'></script>

                  
  

  <!-- UTM parameter handling for forms -->
  <script>
    function getUTMParameterFromCookie(parameterName) {
      // Get the cookie value
      const cookies = document.cookie.split(';');
      let gtmCampaignURL = null;

      for (let i = 0; i < cookies.length; i += 1) {
        const cookie = cookies[i].trim();
        if (cookie.startsWith('__gtm_campaign_url=')) {
          gtmCampaignURL = decodeURIComponent(cookie.substring('__gtm_campaign_url='.length));
          break;
        }
      }

      if (!gtmCampaignURL) {
        return null;
      }

      // Parse the URL to extract the specified query parameter
      const url = new URL(gtmCampaignURL);
      const parameterValue = url.searchParams.get(parameterName);

      return parameterValue || 'null';
    }

    // Retrieve UTM parameter values
    const utmSource = getUTMParameterFromCookie('utm_source');
    const utmMedium = getUTMParameterFromCookie('utm_medium');
    const utmCampaign = getUTMParameterFromCookie('utm_campaign');
    const utmContent = getUTMParameterFromCookie('utm_content');
    const utmTerm = getUTMParameterFromCookie('utm_term');

    // Set the values in the OLAF form inputs if they exist
    const formInputUtmSource = document.getElementById('form-input-utm_source');
    if (formInputUtmSource) {
      formInputUtmSource.value = utmSource || 'null';
    }

    const formInputUtmMedium = document.getElementById('form-input-utm_medium');
    if (formInputUtmMedium) {
      formInputUtmMedium.value = utmMedium || 'null';
    }

    const formInputUtmCampaign = document.getElementById('form-input-utm_campaign');
    if (formInputUtmCampaign) {
      formInputUtmCampaign.value = utmCampaign || 'null';
    }

    const formInputUtmContent = document.getElementById('form-input-utm_content');
    if (formInputUtmContent) {
      formInputUtmContent.value = utmContent || 'null';
    }

    const formInputUtmTerm = document.getElementById('form-input-utm_term');
    if (formInputUtmTerm) {
      formInputUtmTerm.value = utmTerm || 'null';
    }
  </script>

 Home
 

 

 Admissions
 

 

 Payment Options
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 Admissions
 

 
Payment Options

 
We are committed to delivering a fulfilling life beyond addiction. At Caron, we understand that healthcare costs can be a concern. There are several ways our care can be affordable to you, and our team will work with you to find a payment plan that fits your needs.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 

 
 
 

 
 

 
Know your options to afford quality care. 

 Click to learn more. 

Getting treatment for a substance use disorder is not optional. That’s why Caron is committed to helping you find ways to pay for it. Because treatment is personalized for our patients, we do not have a set fee. Some of the variables in the cost of treatment include the specific treatment program that is right for you and the additional services or support you need or choose.

We recommend contacting an admissions specialist to discuss your situation and needs. Our specialists have in-depth knowledge of our programs and the payment options available. If Caron is not the right treatment center for you, we will help you find another quality program.

 

 

 
 
 

 
 

 
How to Pay

We accept certified checks, cash, credit cards (American Express, MasterCard, Visa, and Discover), and wire transfers. Payments can be made upon arrival (with prior approval) or before you arrive by using our secure website or contacting an admissions specialist .

 

 

 
 
 

 
 

 
Treatment Payment Options

At Caron, we believe that everyone deserves access to treatment . We partner with several national and local insurance companies , provide financing, and offer financial assistance to those who need it.

 

 

 
 
 

 
 

 
Financing

Caron has long-standing relationships with two lending companies ( M-lend Financial and Prosper ) that help patients and families pay for treatment. They offer financing for one year or for two years depending upon your eligibility.

Financial Assistance

Caron provides financial assistance to patients who otherwise may not be able to afford treatment. To find out if you are eligible, please talk with one of our admissions specialists.

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
Take the next step:

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 
Start with an online form

                    <h2 class="media-object__heading h2 text-dark">Detox: Your First Step Toward Transformative Treatment</h2>
                    <div class="media-object__paragraph p1 text-neutral"><p>Caron's transformative treatment begins with detox for those who need it. While detox alone does not lead to long-term recovery, it is essential for safely managing the acute withdrawal symptoms associated with drug or alcohol addiction.<br /><br />In Pennsylvania, the detox unit is in the <a href="https://www.caron.org/locations/caron-pennsylvania">Carole and Ray Neag Center</a> with experienced, caring physicians, clinicians, nurse practitioners, nurses, and clinical case managers on duty 24 hours a day to ensure your comfort and evaluate your withdrawal symptoms and physical and mental health. As clinically indicated, they also prescribe withdrawal medications so you are comfortable. <br /><br />In Florida, the detox unit is in <a href="https://www.caron.org/fl-medical-center-ppc">The Keele Center</a> staffed by a full-time, on-site team of expert, compassionate clinicians, physicians, nurse practitioners, nurses, and clinical case managers. The detox care team continuously evaluates your withdrawal symptoms and physical and mental health, prescribing withdrawal medications as clinically indicated so you are comfortable. <br /><br />Both Centers are outfitted to handle the detox process and related conditions, such as chronic pain, diabetes, heart disease, and co-occurring mental health issues. You can rest assured that you will receive the best possible detox care.<br /></p></div>

      <li>
        <button class="two-column-content__toggle two-column-content__toggle--active" id="col1-tab" type="button" role="tab" aria-controls="col1" aria-selected="true">
          Treatment Program
        </button>
      </li>
      <li>
        <button class="two-column-content__toggle" id="col2-tab" type="button" role="tab" aria-controls="col2" aria-selected="false">
          Patient-centered Treatment

    <div class="container-fluid">
    <div class="row">
      <div class="col-md-6 col-lg-8 two-column-content__col two-column-content__col--active" id="col1"  role="tabpanel" aria-labelledby="col1-tab">
        <div class="two-column-content__panel">
          <h2>Treatment Program Details</h2>
<p><a href="https://www.caron.org/blog/what-does-detox-mean">Detoxification</a> neutralizes drugs’ and alcohol’s toxic effects by metabolizing them in the body. Caron manages the detox process carefully to ensure you remain stable. Your team of detox care providers will use their medical and clinical expertise to strengthen your body and mind for treatment.</p><h3>Your Detox Program</h3>
<p>The first step is a comprehensive assessment that includes:</p><ul><li>Blood and urine screenings</li><li>Medical history evaluations</li><li>Psychological assessments</li></ul>
<p>In addition to giving your care providers a full picture of your health, the assessment determines whether medical detox or medication-assisted treatment, such as methadone or anti-craving medications, is necessary. Our medical staff and addiction specialists work together to ensure any prescriptions are medically justified and contribute to your recovery.<br /><br />Caron emphasizes staff/patient relationships as part of its commitment to a caring, respectful environment. The bonds you form with staff are critical to your readiness for treatment and long-term recovery.</p>

<h3>Drugs That May Cause Withdrawal and Require Detox</h3>
<p dir="ltr">While many types of drugs may require detoxification in the treatment process, these are some of the most common substances:</p>
<ul><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr"><a href="https://www.caron.org/addiction-101/alcohol-addiction/what-is-alcohol-abuse-disorder"><strong>Alcohol</strong></a><strong>:</strong> Withdrawal from alcohol can be dangerous and even life-threatening without medical supervision.</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr"><a href="https://www.caron.org/addiction-101/drug-use/what-is-an-opioid"><strong>Opioids</strong></a><strong>:</strong> This includes prescription opioids like oxycodone, as well as heroin and other opiates.</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr"><a href="https://www.caron.org/addiction-101/drug-use/what-are-benzos"><strong>Benzodiazepines</strong></a><strong>:</strong> Drugs like Xanax and Valium require careful tapering and supervision during detox.</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr"><a href="https://www.caron.org/addiction-101/drug-use/what-are-stimulants"><strong>Stimulants</strong></a><strong>:</strong> Cocaine, methamphetamines, and other stimulants cause intense cravings and withdrawal symptoms that can last for weeks.</p></li></ul>
<p dir="ltr">Each of these substances comes with its own set of withdrawal challenges, which makes medically supervised detox critical for safety and effective symptom management.<br /></p>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="col-md-6 col-lg-4 two-column-content__col" id="col2" role="tabpanel" aria-labelledby="col2-tab">
        <div class="two-column-content__panel two-column-content__panel--secondary">
          <h2>Specialized Services</h2><p><strong>For </strong><a href="https://www.caron.org/treating-whole-person/gambling-services"><strong>Gambling Disorder</strong></a></p><ul><li>Treatment based on gambling as a disorder, rather than a moral failing</li><li>Group therapy</li><li>Process group</li><li>12-step group</li><li>Educational presentations</li></ul><p><strong>For </strong><a href="https://www.caron.org/treating-whole-person/lgbtqia-affirming"><strong>LGBTQIA+ Patients</strong></a></p><ul><li>A safe and affirming environment</li><li>Pennsylvania clinicians certified as LGBTQIA+ affirmative and informed by the International Institute for Trauma and Addiction Professionals</li><li>LGBTQIA+-specific groups</li></ul><p>For Patients in the <a href="https://www.caron.org/treatment-programs/legal-professionals">Legal Profession</a></p><ul><li>Individual and group meetings with the legal services coordinator to discuss concerns, including return to work or school</li></ul><p>For <a href="https://www.caron.org/treating-whole-person/physical-wellness">Health and Wellness</a></p><ul><li>Outdoor pool (Florida only)</li><li>Physical and massage therapy as clinically indicated</li><li>Nutritional counseling with varying options depending on the program</li><li>Delicious, healthy food with accommodations for religious guidelines, food allergies, addictions, or eating disorders</li></ul><p>For <a href="https://www.caron.org/treatment-programs/chronic-pain-addiction-treatment">Chronic Pain</a></p><ul><li>Education and pain management counseling</li><li>Medication management</li><li>Mobility accommodation</li><li>Holistic therapies</li><li>Specialized treatments</li></ul>
        </div>

                      <h2 class="intro-context__heading h2">FAQs About Detox</h2>

<div class="flexible-content flexible-content--faq">
  
                                                                                                                                                                              
                                                                        
      
                                                                                            
        <script type="application/ld+json">{
    "@context": "https:\/\/schema.org",
    "@type": "FAQPage",
    "mainEntity": [
        {
            "@type": "Question",
            "name": "How long does the detox process take?",
            "acceptedAnswer": {
                "@type": "Answer",
                "text": "<p dir=\"ltr\">There is no standard time frame for the detoxification process, as it depends on numerous factors. The length of detox and the physical symptoms experienced throughout the process depend on several factors, such as:<br \/><\/p>\n<ul><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">The specific drug that has been used<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Length of addiction<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Amount of drug used during addiction<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Dose taken prior to entering treatment or detox<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Any co-occurring mental health issues<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Use of other substances (including alcohol) during addiction and immediately before drug detox<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Length the specific drug stays in one\u2019s system (half-life of a drug)<\/p><\/li><\/ul>"
            }
        },
        {
            "@type": "Question",
            "name": "What is alcohol and drug detox?",
            "acceptedAnswer": {
                "@type": "Answer",
                "text": "<p>Detox refers to the process of metabolizing drugs and alcohol in a person's system to neutralize their toxic effects. The medical term for detox is \u201cwithdrawal management,\u201d because when the process is managed correctly, detox helps patients maintain physical comfort as they safely withdraw from the effects of drugs and alcohol. It is important for detox to be medically supervised at a detox addiction treatment center or in a drug detoxification program, where doctors and healthcare experts are on-site to provide supervision, observations and support to manage symptoms, stabilize the patient\u2019s health and keep patients safe from the harmful physical complications of intoxication and withdrawal.<\/p>"
            }
        },
        {
            "@type": "Question",
            "name": "What are the side effects of withdrawal and detox?",
            "acceptedAnswer": {
                "@type": "Answer",
                "text": "<p>Regardless of whether a person is entering inpatient alcohol treatment, inpatient drug treatment, or outpatient drug or alcohol rehab, safely managing the withdrawal process is a critical component of treatment and care. The side effects of detox from alcohol and drug addiction can vary depending on the type of drug or drugs a person has in their system, how long they\u2019ve been using, the state of their general overall health, and the like. The process is different for each person. See the next questions for the side effects of withdrawal from alcohol and specific types of drugs.<\/p>"
            }
        },
        {
            "@type": "Question",
            "name": "What are the side effects of alcohol withdrawal?",
            "acceptedAnswer": {
                "@type": "Answer",
                "text": "<p>Alcohol withdrawal is the most dangerous because physically detoxing from alcohol can be fatal. It can also cause grand mal seizures, strokes, and heart attacks. Because of these risks, alcohol detox should always occur in a medically supervised setting with close monitoring. Other side effects of alcohol detox include hallucinations, delirium tremens (DTs), anxiety, nausea, insomnia, increased body temperature, heart palpitations, abdominal pain, and more.<\/p>"
            }
        },
        {
            "@type": "Question",
            "name": "What are the side effects of opioid withdrawal?",
            "acceptedAnswer": {
                "@type": "Answer",
                "text": "<p dir=\"ltr\">Opioids include opiates like morphine, heroin, and codeine, as well as synthetic opioids like oxycodone and hydrocodone, which are used to manage pain. Opioid detox side effects can vary greatly from person to person, depending on the type of opioid used and the duration of dependence. In general, though, opioid detox side effects can be broken down into two stages. Comfort medication is commonly used in opioid drug detoxification and opioid addiction rehab programs, as the experience after the first 24 hours can be incredibly unpleasant.<\/p>\n<p dir=\"ltr\"><strong>Early-stage <\/strong><strong>opioid<\/strong><strong> withdrawal side effects<\/strong> (usually within 24 hours of a person\u2019s last opioid use \u2014 and often as soon as 12 hours post-use):<\/p>\n<ul><li><p dir=\"ltr\">Aggression<\/p><\/li><li><p dir=\"ltr\">Anxiety<\/p><\/li><li><p dir=\"ltr\">Anger<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Aggression<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Delusions<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Intense cravings for the drug<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Restless legs<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Sweating<\/p><\/li><\/ul>\n<p dir=\"ltr\"><strong>Later-stage <\/strong><strong>opioid<\/strong><strong> withdrawal side effects<\/strong> (24 hours or more post-use):<br \/><\/p>\n<ul><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Abdominal cramps<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Blurry vision<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Diarrhea<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Dilated pupils<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">High blood pressure<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Nausea<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Racing heartbeat<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Vomiting<\/p><\/li><\/ul>"
            }
        },
        {
            "@type": "Question",
            "name": "What are the side effects of benzodiazepines withdrawal?",
            "acceptedAnswer": {
                "@type": "Answer",
                "text": "<p>Detoxing from benzodiazepines can be dangerous and should only be done under medical supervision. Physical withdrawal can take months to complete, so choosing a drug detoxification program and treatment program can greatly reduce discomfort and relapse.<\/p>\n<p>Because different benzodiazepines have different half-lives, side effect symptoms and timelines can vary, but in general, benzodiazepine detox side effects include:<\/p>\n<ul><li>Anxiety<br \/><\/li><li>Heart palpitations<\/li><li>Insomnia<\/li><li>Irritability<\/li><li>Sour stomach<\/li><li>Tremors<\/li><li>Weight loss<\/li><\/ul>"
            }
        },
        {
            "@type": "Question",
            "name": "What are the side effects of withdrawal from cocaine, meth and other stimulants?",
            "acceptedAnswer": {
                "@type": "Answer",
                "text": "<p dir=\"ltr\">Side effects from a stimulant like cocaine, crack cocaine, or methamphetamines usually start within 36 hours of stopping the drug and can continue for a few weeks or even months. Withdrawing from cocaine use and other forms of stimulant use is uncomfortable, and the symptoms are difficult to manage. Comfort medication is often used in cocaine and methamphetamine treatment.<\/p>\n<p dir=\"ltr\">Common side effects that occur when detoxing from stimulants can be broken down into three stages.<\/p>\n<p dir=\"ltr\"><strong>Early Stage (or \"crashing\"):<\/strong><\/p>\n<ul><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Aggression<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Agitation<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Anger<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Anxiety<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Delusions<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Fatigue<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Increased appetite<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Intense drug cravings<\/p><\/li><\/ul>\n<p dir=\"ltr\"><strong>Middle Stage:<\/strong><br \/><\/p>\n<ul><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Confusion<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Cravings<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Depression<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Fatigue<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Inability to focus<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Insomnia<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Memory loss<\/p><\/li><\/ul>\n<p dir=\"ltr\"><strong>Late Stage:<\/strong><br \/><\/p>\n<ul><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Bad dreams<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Extreme appetite<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Intense sleepiness<\/p><\/li><li dir=\"ltr\"><p dir=\"ltr\">Mood swings<\/p><\/li><\/ul>"
            }
        }
    ]
}</script>

        <article class="accordion__panel" data-accordion-panel>
      <h3 class="accordion__header">
        <button class="accordion__button" data-accordion-button aria-expanded="false">
          How long does the detox process take?
          <div class="accordion__signal"></div>

      <div class="accordion__window" data-accordion-window>
        <div class="accordion__content wysiwyg" data-accordion-content>
                    <div class="wysiwyg container-fluid">
  <p dir="ltr">There is no standard time frame for the detoxification process, as it depends on numerous factors. The length of detox and the physical symptoms experienced throughout the process depend on several factors, such as:<br /></p>
<ul><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">The specific drug that has been used</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Length of addiction</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Amount of drug used during addiction</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Dose taken prior to entering treatment or detox</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Any co-occurring mental health issues</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Use of other substances (including alcohol) during addiction and immediately before drug detox</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Length the specific drug stays in one’s system (half-life of a drug)</p></li></ul>
</div>
                  </div>
      </div>
    </article>
        <article class="accordion__panel" data-accordion-panel>
      <h3 class="accordion__header">
        <button class="accordion__button" data-accordion-button aria-expanded="false">
          What is alcohol and drug detox?
          <div class="accordion__signal"></div>
        </button>
      </h3>
      <div class="accordion__window" data-accordion-window>
        <div class="accordion__content wysiwyg" data-accordion-content>
                    <div class="wysiwyg container-fluid">
  <p>Detox refers to the process of metabolizing drugs and alcohol in a person's system to neutralize their toxic effects. The medical term for detox is “withdrawal management,” because when the process is managed correctly, detox helps patients maintain physical comfort as they safely withdraw from the effects of drugs and alcohol. It is important for detox to be medically supervised at a detox addiction treatment center or in a drug detoxification program, where doctors and healthcare experts are on-site to provide supervision, observations and support to manage symptoms, stabilize the patient’s health and keep patients safe from the harmful physical complications of intoxication and withdrawal.</p>
</div>
                  </div>
      </div>
    </article>
        <article class="accordion__panel" data-accordion-panel>
      <h3 class="accordion__header">
        <button class="accordion__button" data-accordion-button aria-expanded="false">

        <div class="accordion__content wysiwyg" data-accordion-content>
                    <div class="wysiwyg container-fluid">
  <p>Regardless of whether a person is entering inpatient alcohol treatment, inpatient drug treatment, or outpatient drug or alcohol rehab, safely managing the withdrawal process is a critical component of treatment and care. The side effects of detox from alcohol and drug addiction can vary depending on the type of drug or drugs a person has in their system, how long they’ve been using, the state of their general overall health, and the like. The process is different for each person. See the next questions for the side effects of withdrawal from alcohol and specific types of drugs.</p>
</div>
                  </div>
      </div>
    </article>
        <article class="accordion__panel" data-accordion-panel>
      <h3 class="accordion__header">
        <button class="accordion__button" data-accordion-button aria-expanded="false">
          What are the side effects of alcohol withdrawal?
          <div class="accordion__signal"></div>
        </button>
      </h3>
      <div class="accordion__window" data-accordion-window>
        <div class="accordion__content wysiwyg" data-accordion-content>
                    <div class="wysiwyg container-fluid">
  <p>Alcohol withdrawal is the most dangerous because physically detoxing from alcohol can be fatal. It can also cause grand mal seizures, strokes, and heart attacks. Because of these risks, alcohol detox should always occur in a medically supervised setting with close monitoring. Other side effects of alcohol detox include hallucinations, delirium tremens (DTs), anxiety, nausea, insomnia, increased body temperature, heart palpitations, abdominal pain, and more.</p>
</div>
                  </div>
      </div>
    </article>
        <article class="accordion__panel" data-accordion-panel>
      <h3 class="accordion__header">
        <button class="accordion__button" data-accordion-button aria-expanded="false">
          What are the side effects of opioid withdrawal?
          <div class="accordion__signal"></div>
        </button>
      </h3>
      <div class="accordion__window" data-accordion-window>
        <div class="accordion__content wysiwyg" data-accordion-content>
                    <div class="wysiwyg container-fluid">
  <p dir="ltr">Opioids include opiates like morphine, heroin, and codeine, as well as synthetic opioids like oxycodone and hydrocodone, which are used to manage pain. Opioid detox side effects can vary greatly from person to person, depending on the type of opioid used and the duration of dependence. In general, though, opioid detox side effects can be broken down into two stages. Comfort medication is commonly used in opioid drug detoxification and opioid addiction rehab programs, as the experience after the first 24 hours can be incredibly unpleasant.</p>
<p dir="ltr"><strong>Early-stage </strong><strong>opioid</strong><strong> withdrawal side effects</strong> (usually within 24 hours of a person’s last opioid use — and often as soon as 12 hours post-use):</p>
<ul><li><p dir="ltr">Aggression</p></li><li><p dir="ltr">Anxiety</p></li><li><p dir="ltr">Anger</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Aggression</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Delusions</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Intense cravings for the drug</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Restless legs</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Sweating</p></li></ul>
<p dir="ltr"><strong>Later-stage </strong><strong>opioid</strong><strong> withdrawal side effects</strong> (24 hours or more post-use):<br /></p>
<ul><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Abdominal cramps</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Blurry vision</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Diarrhea</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Dilated pupils</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">High blood pressure</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Nausea</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Racing heartbeat</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Vomiting</p></li></ul>
</div>
                  </div>
      </div>
    </article>
        <article class="accordion__panel" data-accordion-panel>
      <h3 class="accordion__header">
        <button class="accordion__button" data-accordion-button aria-expanded="false">
          What are the side effects of benzodiazepines withdrawal?
          <div class="accordion__signal"></div>
        </button>
      </h3>
      <div class="accordion__window" data-accordion-window>
        <div class="accordion__content wysiwyg" data-accordion-content>
                    <div class="wysiwyg container-fluid">
  <p>Detoxing from benzodiazepines can be dangerous and should only be done under medical supervision. Physical withdrawal can take months to complete, so choosing a drug detoxification program and treatment program can greatly reduce discomfort and relapse.</p>
<p>Because different benzodiazepines have different half-lives, side effect symptoms and timelines can vary, but in general, benzodiazepine detox side effects include:</p>
<ul><li>Anxiety<br /></li><li>Heart palpitations</li><li>Insomnia</li><li>Irritability</li><li>Sour stomach</li><li>Tremors</li><li>Weight loss</li></ul>
</div>
                  </div>
      </div>
    </article>
        <article class="accordion__panel" data-accordion-panel>
      <h3 class="accordion__header">
        <button class="accordion__button" data-accordion-button aria-expanded="false">
          What are the side effects of withdrawal from cocaine, meth and other stimulants?
          <div class="accordion__signal"></div>
        </button>
      </h3>
      <div class="accordion__window" data-accordion-window>
        <div class="accordion__content wysiwyg" data-accordion-content>
                    <div class="wysiwyg container-fluid">
  <p dir="ltr">Side effects from a stimulant like cocaine, crack cocaine, or methamphetamines usually start within 36 hours of stopping the drug and can continue for a few weeks or even months. Withdrawing from cocaine use and other forms of stimulant use is uncomfortable, and the symptoms are difficult to manage. Comfort medication is often used in cocaine and methamphetamine treatment.</p>
<p dir="ltr">Common side effects that occur when detoxing from stimulants can be broken down into three stages.</p>
<p dir="ltr"><strong>Early Stage (or "crashing"):</strong></p>
<ul><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Aggression</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Agitation</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Anger</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Anxiety</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Delusions</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Fatigue</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Increased appetite</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Intense drug cravings</p></li></ul>
<p dir="ltr"><strong>Middle Stage:</strong><br /></p>
<ul><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Confusion</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Cravings</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Depression</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Fatigue</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Inability to focus</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Insomnia</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Memory loss</p></li></ul>
<p dir="ltr"><strong>Late Stage:</strong><br /></p>
<ul><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Bad dreams</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Extreme appetite</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Intense sleepiness</p></li><li dir="ltr"><p dir="ltr">Mood swings</p></li></ul>
</div>
                  </div>
      </div>
    </article>
      </div>
</div>

  <!-- UTM parameter handling for forms -->
  <script>
    function getUTMParameterFromCookie(parameterName) {
      // Get the cookie value
      const cookies = document.cookie.split(';');
      let gtmCampaignURL = null;

      for (let i = 0; i < cookies.length; i += 1) {
        const cookie = cookies[i].trim();
        if (cookie.startsWith('__gtm_campaign_url=')) {
          gtmCampaignURL = decodeURIComponent(cookie.substring('__gtm_campaign_url='.length));
          break;
        }
      }

      if (!gtmCampaignURL) {
        return null;
      }

      // Parse the URL to extract the specified query parameter
      const url = new URL(gtmCampaignURL);
      const parameterValue = url.searchParams.get(parameterName);

      return parameterValue || 'null';
    }

    // Retrieve UTM parameter values
    const utmSource = getUTMParameterFromCookie('utm_source');
    const utmMedium = getUTMParameterFromCookie('utm_medium');
    const utmCampaign = getUTMParameterFromCookie('utm_campaign');
    const utmContent = getUTMParameterFromCookie('utm_content');
    const utmTerm = getUTMParameterFromCookie('utm_term');

    // Set the values in the OLAF form inputs if they exist
    const formInputUtmSource = document.getElementById('form-input-utm_source');
    if (formInputUtmSource) {
      formInputUtmSource.value = utmSource || 'null';
    }

    const formInputUtmMedium = document.getElementById('form-input-utm_medium');
    if (formInputUtmMedium) {
      formInputUtmMedium.value = utmMedium || 'null';
    }

    const formInputUtmCampaign = document.getElementById('form-input-utm_campaign');
    if (formInputUtmCampaign) {
      formInputUtmCampaign.value = utmCampaign || 'null';
    }

    const formInputUtmContent = document.getElementById('form-input-utm_content');
    if (formInputUtmContent) {
      formInputUtmContent.value = utmContent || 'null';
    }

    const formInputUtmTerm = document.getElementById('form-input-utm_term');
    if (formInputUtmTerm) {
      formInputUtmTerm.value = utmTerm || 'null';
    }
  </script>

 Drug and Alcohol Detox Programs
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 
Drug and Alcohol Detox Programs

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 

 Locations:
 
 

 Pennsylvania 
 
, 

 Florida 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 

 
 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 Previous slide 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 Next slide 
 

 
 
 
 
 

 
The Keele Center

 

 
 
 

 

 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 Previous slide 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 Next slide 
 

 
 
 
 
 

 
Detox services in the Keele Center

 

 
 
 

 

 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 Previous slide

Neag Medical Center

 

 
 
 

 

 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 Previous slide 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 Next slide 
 

 
 
 
 
 

 
Detox services in the Neag Medical Center

 

 
 
 

 

 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 Previous slide 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 Next slide 
 

 
 
 
 
 

 
Detox unit bedroom in the Neag Medical Center

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Detox: Your First Step Toward Transformative Treatment

 

Caron's transformative treatment begins with detox for those who need it. While detox alone does not lead to long-term recovery, it is essential for safely managing the acute withdrawal symptoms associated with drug or alcohol addiction.

In Pennsylvania, the detox unit is in the Carole and Ray Neag Center with experienced, caring physicians, clinicians, nurse practitioners, nurses, and clinical case managers on duty 24 hours a day to ensure your comfort and evaluate your withdrawal symptoms and physical and mental health. As clinically indicated, they also prescribe withdrawal medications so you are comfortable. 

In Florida, the detox unit is in The Keele Center staffed by a full-time, on-site team of expert, compassionate clinicians, physicians, nurse practitioners, nurses, and clinical case managers. The detox care team continuously evaluates your withdrawal symptoms and physical and mental health, prescribing withdrawal medications as clinically indicated so you are comfortable. 

Both Centers are outfitted to handle the detox process and related conditions, such as chronic pain, diabetes, heart disease, and co-occurring mental health issues. You can rest assured that you will receive the best possible detox care.

 

 

 

 
 

 
 

 
 
 
 Close 
 
 
Detox: Your First Step Toward Transformative Treatment

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 Previous slide 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 Next slide 
 

 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 

 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 Previous slide 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 Next slide 
 

 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 

 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 Previous slide 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 Next slide 
 

 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 

 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 Previous slide 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 Next slide 
 

 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 

 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 Previous slide 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 Next slide 
 

 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 
 

 
 
 

 
 
 
 

 
 
 

 
 Treatment Program
 
 

 

 
 Patient-centered Treatment
 
 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 
Treatment Program Details

 Detoxification neutralizes drugs’ and alcohol’s toxic effects by metabolizing them in the body. Caron manages the detox process carefully to ensure you remain stable. Your team of detox care providers will use their medical and clinical expertise to strengthen your body and mind for treatment.

Your Detox Program

The first step is a comprehensive assessment that includes:
 
Blood and urine screenings

Medical history evaluations

Psychological assessments
 

In addition to giving your care providers a full picture of your health, the assessment determines whether medical detox or medication-assisted treatment, such as methadone or anti-craving medications, is necessary. Our medical staff and addiction specialists work together to ensure any prescriptions are medically justified and contribute to your recovery.

Caron emphasizes staff/patient relationships as part of its commitment to a caring, respectful environment. The bonds you form with staff are critical to your readiness for treatment and long-term recovery.

Drugs That May Cause Withdrawal and Require Detox

While many types of drugs may require detoxification in the treatment process, these are some of the most common substances:

 

 Alcohol : Withdrawal from alcohol can be dangerous and even life-threatening without medical supervision.

 Opioids : This includes prescription opioids like oxycodone, as well as heroin and other opiates.

 Benzodiazepines : Drugs like Xanax and Valium require careful tapering and supervision during detox.

 Stimulants : Cocaine, methamphetamines, and other stimulants cause intense cravings and withdrawal symptoms that can last for weeks.

 

Each of these substances comes with its own set of withdrawal challenges, which makes medically supervised detox critical for safety and effective symptom management.

 

 

 

 

 
Specialized Services

 For Gambling Disorder 
 
Treatment based on gambling as a disorder, rather than a moral failing

Group therapy

Process group

12-step group

Educational presentations
 
 For LGBTQIA+ Patients 
 
A safe and affirming environment

Pennsylvania clinicians certified as LGBTQIA+ affirmative and informed by the International Institute for Trauma and Addiction Professionals

LGBTQIA+-specific groups
 
For Patients in the Legal Profession 
 
Individual and group meetings with the legal services coordinator to discuss concerns, including return to work or school
 
For Health and Wellness 
 
Outdoor pool (Florida only)

Physical and massage therapy as clinically indicated

Nutritional counseling with varying options depending on the program

Delicious, healthy food with accommodations for religious guidelines, food allergies, addictions, or eating disorders
 
For Chronic Pain 
 
Education and pain management counseling

Medication management

Mobility accommodation

Holistic therapies

Specialized treatments
 
 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 
One of America's Best Addiction Treatment Centers 2023

 
 Read Our Blog
 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 
 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 
 
Your care is the heart of Caron’s visionary mission.

 
 

 
 

 

 
 

 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 
Dr. Adam D. Scioli, D.O., DFAPA, FASAM, FAOAAM

 
Chief Medical Officer and Program Director, Caron/Tower Addiction Medicine Fellowship

 
Pennsylvania, Florida

 

 
 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 
Dr. Ming R. Wang, MD, FASAM

 
Medical Director, Caron Pennsylvania

 
Pennsylvania

 

 
 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 
Barbara Krantz, D.O., MS, DFASAM, MRO

 
Medical Director of the Neurocognitive Assessment Program

 
Florida

 

 
 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 
Amanda Peña, BS, CAAP

 
Associate Behavioral Health Therapist

 
Pennsylvania

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 

 

 
 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 
 
FAQs About Detox

 
 

 
 

 

 
 

 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 
 How long does the detox process take?
 

 
 

 

 

 

 
There is no standard time frame for the detoxification process, as it depends on numerous factors. The length of detox and the physical symptoms experienced throughout the process depend on several factors, such as:

 

The specific drug that has been used

Length of addiction

Amount of drug used during addiction

Dose taken prior to entering treatment or detox

Any co-occurring mental health issues

Use of other substances (including alcohol) during addiction and immediately before drug detox

Length the specific drug stays in one’s system (half-life of a drug)

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 What is alcohol and drug detox?
 

 
 

 

 

 

 
Detox refers to the process of metabolizing drugs and alcohol in a person's system to neutralize their toxic effects. The medical term for detox is “withdrawal management,” because when the process is managed correctly, detox helps patients maintain physical comfort as they safely withdraw from the effects of drugs and alcohol. It is important for detox to be medically supervised at a detox addiction treatment center or in a drug detoxification program, where doctors and healthcare experts are on-site to provide supervision, observations and support to manage symptoms, stabilize the patient’s health and keep patients safe from the harmful physical complications of intoxication and withdrawal.

 

 

 

 

 

 
 What are the side effects of withdrawal and detox?
 

 
 

 

 

 

 
Regardless of whether a person is entering inpatient alcohol treatment, inpatient drug treatment, or outpatient drug or alcohol rehab, safely managing the withdrawal process is a critical component of treatment and care. The side effects of detox from alcohol and drug addiction can vary depending on the type of drug or drugs a person has in their system, how long they’ve been using, the state of their general overall health, and the like. The process is different for each person. See the next questions for the side effects of withdrawal from alcohol and specific types of drugs.

 

 

 

 

 

 
 What are the side effects of alcohol withdrawal?
 

 
 

 

 

 

 
Alcohol withdrawal is the most dangerous because physically detoxing from alcohol can be fatal. It can also cause grand mal seizures, strokes, and heart attacks. Because of these risks, alcohol detox should always occur in a medically supervised setting with close monitoring. Other side effects of alcohol detox include hallucinations, delirium tremens (DTs), anxiety, nausea, insomnia, increased body temperature, heart palpitations, abdominal pain, and more.

 

 

 

 

 

 
 What are the side effects of opioid withdrawal?
 

 
 

 

 

 

 
Opioids include opiates like morphine, heroin, and codeine, as well as synthetic opioids like oxycodone and hydrocodone, which are used to manage pain. Opioid detox side effects can vary greatly from person to person, depending on the type of opioid used and the duration of dependence. In general, though, opioid detox side effects can be broken down into two stages. Comfort medication is commonly used in opioid drug detoxification and opioid addiction rehab programs, as the experience after the first 24 hours can be incredibly unpleasant.

 Early-stage opioid withdrawal side effects (usually within 24 hours of a person’s last opioid use — and often as soon as 12 hours post-use):

 

Aggression

Anxiety

Anger

Aggression

Delusions

Intense cravings for the drug

Restless legs

Sweating

 

 Later-stage opioid withdrawal side effects (24 hours or more post-use):

 

Abdominal cramps

Blurry vision

Diarrhea

Dilated pupils

High blood pressure

Nausea

Racing heartbeat

Vomiting

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 What are the side effects of benzodiazepines withdrawal?
 

 
 

 

 

 

 
Detoxing from benzodiazepines can be dangerous and should only be done under medical supervision. Physical withdrawal can take months to complete, so choosing a drug detoxification program and treatment program can greatly reduce discomfort and relapse.

Because different benzodiazepines have different half-lives, side effect symptoms and timelines can vary, but in general, benzodiazepine detox side effects include:

 
Anxiety

Heart palpitations

Insomnia

Irritability

Sour stomach

Tremors

Weight loss
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 What are the side effects of withdrawal from cocaine, meth and other stimulants?
 

 
 

 

 

 

 
Side effects from a stimulant like cocaine, crack cocaine, or methamphetamines usually start within 36 hours of stopping the drug and can continue for a few weeks or even months. Withdrawing from cocaine use and other forms of stimulant use is uncomfortable, and the symptoms are difficult to manage. Comfort medication is often used in cocaine and methamphetamine treatment.

Common side effects that occur when detoxing from stimulants can be broken down into three stages.

 Early Stage (or "crashing"): 

 

Aggression

Agitation

Anger

Anxiety

Delusions

Fatigue

Increased appetite

Intense drug cravings

 

 Middle Stage: 

 

Confusion

Cravings

Depression

Fatigue

Inability to focus

Insomnia

Memory loss

 

 Late Stage: 

 

Bad dreams

Extreme appetite

Intense sleepiness

Mood swings

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
Take the next step:

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 
 

 
Start with an online form

<div class="flexible-content flexible-content--body">
  
                                                                                                                                                                                  <div class="wysiwyg container-fluid">
  <p dir="ltr">Mental health challenges can feel overwhelming, but you’re not alone. Many people experience struggles like persistent worry, low mood, or difficulty navigating daily life. These experiences don’t define you — they’re a sign that you may need support, and that’s okay.</p>
<p>Taking the time to understand mental health disorders can be empowering. Whether it’s for yourself or someone you care about, learning about the causes, symptoms, and treatment options is an important step. Recovery is possible, and with the right care, you can find balance and joy again.</p>
</div>
  

    

</div>
  
    
    
<div class="flexible-content flexible-content--body">
  
                                                                                                                                                                                  <div class="wysiwyg container-fluid">
  <h2>What Are Mental Health Disorders?</h2>
<p dir="ltr">Mental health disorders are medical conditions that affect a person’s thoughts, emotions, behaviors or overall functioning. They can disrupt daily life, relationships and work but are treatable with the right care and support. These conditions are not a sign of weakness — they are complex and influenced by various biological, environmental and psychological factors.</p>
<p dir="ltr">Mental health disorders are more common than many realize. Millions of people are affected each year, spanning all ages, backgrounds and lifestyles. Understanding that mental health challenges are widespread can help normalize the conversation and encourage those affected to seek help.</p>
<p>When individuals recognize the signs and symptoms of mental health challenges, they can take steps to seek care before the condition worsens. Caron offers specialized support for co-occurring mental health and substance use disorders at its <a href="https://www.caron.org/locations/caron-pennsylvania">Pennsylvania location</a> and <a href="https://www.caron.org/locations/caron-florida">Florida location</a>. Supporting mental health also creates a compassionate environment where individuals feel empowered to reach out for assistance without fear of judgment.</p>
</div>
  

    

</div>
  
    
    
<div class="flexible-content flexible-content--body">
  
                                                                                                                                                                                  <div class="wysiwyg container-fluid">
  <h2>Causes of Mental Health Disorders</h2>
<p>Mental health disorders often stem from a complex combination of influences, making each individual’s experience unique. Common causes include:</p>
</div>
  

    

</div>
  
    
    
<div class="flexible-content flexible-content--checkList">
  
                                                                                                                                                                              
                                                    
  <div class="container-fluid">
  <div class="row">
    <div class="col-12">
              <ul class="check-list">
                    <li>
            <strong>Genetics and Brain Chemistry:</strong> A family history of mental health conditions or imbalances in brain chemicals can increase vulnerability to disorders.<br />
            <span class="check-list__decor">
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-icon_check"></use>
</svg>
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-bubble-mini-5"></use>
</svg>
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-bubble-mini-4"></use>
</svg>
            </span>
          </li>
                    <li>
            <strong>Trauma and Stress:</strong> Experiencing traumatic events, chronic stress or significant life changes — such as loss, abuse or instability — can contribute to mental health challenges.<br />
            <span class="check-list__decor">
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-icon_check"></use>
</svg>
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-bubble-mini-5"></use>
</svg>
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-bubble-mini-4"></use>
</svg>
            </span>
          </li>
                    <li>
            <strong>Underlying Physical Health Issues:</strong> Conditions like chronic pain, hormonal imbalances or other medical problems can impact mental well-being.<br />
            <span class="check-list__decor">
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-icon_check"></use>
</svg>
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-bubble-mini-5"></use>
</svg>
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-bubble-mini-4"></use>
</svg>
            </span>
          </li>
                    <li>
            <p dir="ltr"><strong>Unresolved Emotional Difficulties:</strong> Struggles with emotional regulation, self-esteem or unresolved past experiences can lead to mental health concerns over time.</p>
            <span class="check-list__decor">
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-icon_check"></use>
</svg>
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-bubble-mini-5"></use>
</svg>
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-bubble-mini-4"></use>
</svg>
            </span>
          </li>
                    <li>
            <strong>Co-Occurring Conditions:</strong> Mental health disorders often occur alongside other issues, such as substance use disorders, compounding their effects.<br />
            <span class="check-list__decor">
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-icon_check"></use>
</svg>
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-bubble-mini-5"></use>
</svg>
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-bubble-mini-4"></use>
</svg>
            </span>
          </li>
                  </ul>
          </div>
  </div>
</div>


    

</div>
  
    
    
<div class="flexible-content flexible-content--body">
  
                                                                                                                                                                                  <div class="wysiwyg container-fluid">
  <h2>Common Types of Mental Health Disorders</h2>
<p>Mental health disorders encompass a wide range of conditions, each with unique characteristics and challenges.</p><h3>Anxiety Disorders</h3>
</div>
  

    

</div>
  
    
    
<div class="flexible-content flexible-content--checkList">
  
                                                                                                                                                                              
                                
  <div class="container-fluid">
  <div class="row">
    <div class="col-12">
              <ul class="check-list">
                    <li>
            <p dir="ltr"><a href="https://www.caron.org/addiction-101/treatment-advice/mental-health-disorders"><strong>Generalized </strong><strong>Anxiety Disorder</strong></a><strong> (GAD):</strong> Persistent and excessive worry about everyday situations, often accompanied by physical symptoms like restlessness, fatigue or difficulty concentrating. Excessive worry can significantly impact daily life and relationships, highlighting the importance of treatment.</p>
            <span class="check-list__decor">
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-icon_check"></use>
</svg>
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-bubble-mini-5"></use>
</svg>
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-bubble-mini-4"></use>
</svg>
            </span>
          </li>
                    <li>
            <strong>Panic Disorder</strong><strong>:</strong> Sudden, intense episodes of fear accompanied by physical symptoms like a racing heart.<br />
            <span class="check-list__decor">
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-icon_check"></use>
</svg>
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-bubble-mini-5"></use>
</svg>
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-bubble-mini-4"></use>
</svg>
            </span>
          </li>
                    <li>
            <strong>Social Anxiety Disorder</strong><strong>:</strong> Extreme fear of social situations, leading to avoidance or distress and often tied to feelings of low self-esteem.<br />
            <span class="check-list__decor">
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-icon_check"></use>
</svg>
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-bubble-mini-5"></use>
</svg>
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-bubble-mini-4"></use>
</svg>
            </span>
          </li>
                  </ul>
          </div>
  </div>
</div>


    

</div>
  
    
    
<div class="flexible-content flexible-content--body">
  
                                                                                                                                                                                  <div class="wysiwyg container-fluid">
  <h3>Mood Disorders (Affective Disorders)</h3>
</div>
  

    

</div>
  
    
    
<div class="flexible-content flexible-content--checkList">
  
                                                                                                                                                                              
                      
  <div class="container-fluid">
  <div class="row">
    <div class="col-12">
              <ul class="check-list">
                    <li>
            <strong>Depression:</strong> Persistent sadness, loss of interest in activities and feelings of hopelessness. In severe cases, depression can lead to suicidal thoughts or actions, making early intervention critical.<br />
            <span class="check-list__decor">
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-icon_check"></use>
</svg>
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">

</svg>
            </span>
          </li>
                    <li>
            <strong>Bipolar Disorder</strong><strong>:</strong> Episodes of extreme highs (mania) and lows (depression) that affect mood and energy.<br />
            <span class="check-list__decor">
              

<svg class="icon  " aria-hidden="true">
  <use xlink:href="/dist/main.svg#u-icon-icon_check"></use>

            </span>
          </li>

  </div>
</div>


    

</div>
  
    
    
<div class="flexible-content flexible-content--body">
  
                                                                                                                                                                                  <div class="wysiwyg container-fluid">

<p dir="ltr">Diagnosing a mental health disorder is a thoughtful and collaborative process designed to provide clarity and support. It starts with meeting a qualified professional, such as a psychiatrist, psychologist or licensed therapist, who will take the time to understand your unique experiences.</p>
<p dir="ltr">The process often includes:<br /></p>

                    <li>

  <!-- UTM parameter handling for forms -->
  <script>
    function getUTMParameterFromCookie(parameterName) {
      // Get the cookie value
      const cookies = document.cookie.split(';');
      let gtmCampaignURL = null;

      for (let i = 0; i < cookies.length; i += 1) {
        const cookie = cookies[i].trim();
        if (cookie.startsWith('__gtm_campaign_url=')) {
          gtmCampaignURL = decodeURIComponent(cookie.substring('__gtm_campaign_url='.length));
          break;
        }
      }

      if (!gtmCampaignURL) {
        return null;
      }

      // Parse the URL to extract the specified query parameter
      const url = new URL(gtmCampaignURL);
      const parameterValue = url.searchParams.get(parameterName);

      return parameterValue || 'null';
    }

    // Retrieve UTM parameter values
    const utmSource = getUTMParameterFromCookie('utm_source');
    const utmMedium = getUTMParameterFromCookie('utm_medium');
    const utmCampaign = getUTMParameterFromCookie('utm_campaign');
    const utmContent = getUTMParameterFromCookie('utm_content');
    const utmTerm = getUTMParameterFromCookie('utm_term');

    // Set the values in the OLAF form inputs if they exist
    const formInputUtmSource = document.getElementById('form-input-utm_source');
    if (formInputUtmSource) {
      formInputUtmSource.value = utmSource || 'null';
    }

    const formInputUtmMedium = document.getElementById('form-input-utm_medium');
    if (formInputUtmMedium) {
      formInputUtmMedium.value = utmMedium || 'null';
    }

    const formInputUtmCampaign = document.getElementById('form-input-utm_campaign');
    if (formInputUtmCampaign) {
      formInputUtmCampaign.value = utmCampaign || 'null';
    }

    const formInputUtmContent = document.getElementById('form-input-utm_content');
    if (formInputUtmContent) {
      formInputUtmContent.value = utmContent || 'null';
    }

    const formInputUtmTerm = document.getElementById('form-input-utm_term');
    if (formInputUtmTerm) {
      formInputUtmTerm.value = utmTerm || 'null';
    }
  </script>
